description: You are a molecular virologist preparing to clone the complete episomal genome of Human Papillomavirus type 16 (HPV16). Because the HPV genome is a ~7.9 kb circular DNA molecule, it must first be 'linearized' by cutting it with a restriction enzyme that cleaves the sequence exactly once. Using the reference genome at ~/UGENE_Data/hpv/hpv16_reference.gb, identify all single-cutter restriction enzymes from the standard database, ensuring the analysis treats the genome as a circular molecule. Generate annotations for these single-cutter sites and export the annotated sequence as a GenBank file to ~/UGENE_Data/hpv/results/hpv16_single_cutters.gb. Additionally, create a plain text report at ~/UGENE_Data/hpv/results/linearization_report.txt that includes the total number of single-cutter enzymes found, a list of at least five identified enzymes (including BamHI and SphI), and a confirmation of whether EcoRI and HindIII are single-cutters with a brief explanation based on their hit counts.

metadata.data_source: NCBI Nucleotide: NC_001526.4 (Human papillomavirus type 16, complete genome)
